methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Base Power is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Zach Dell and Justin Lopas that has raised $50M+. Valuation assessment draws on a comparison set of 9 private companies and 1 public peer, with direct stage and sector matching available for 3 private peers in Climate & Energy at Series A: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression uses the $50M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying $100M. Peer-median triangulation across the 3 same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median of $150M, with a range of $110M to $200M. The methods diverge by $50M, reflecting uncertainty in both the raised-capital proxy and peer selection representativeness.

Synthesizing both approaches supports a valuation range of $85M (low) to $172M (high), with a median point of $125M. The low-end estimate applies a 15% discount to the stage-progression result; the high end reflects the upper quartile of peer valuations. Key uncertainties include whether $50M raised is an appropriate valuation anchor absent a disclosed round price, the degree to which the 3-peer sample reflects Base Power's competitive positioning, and stage-specific risk factors not captured by cross-sectional comparables.
